spec.name: IPLD DAG-CBOR
spec.ordering: Map keys MUST be sorted length-first: shorter UTF-8 key byte-length first, then bytewise lexicographic within equal length (RFC 8949 §4.2.1 deterministic encoding).

vector.name: IPLD codec-fixtures (dag-cbor)
vector.note: Cross-referenced. Expected CIDs below were independently produced by a pure-Python DAG-CBOR encoder that implements length-first canonical map-key ordering directly from the IPLD spec / RFC 8949 §4.2.1 (NOT via the Rust impl). The hello-world and empty-map literals additionally match the widely published dag-cbor reference vectors in js-multiformats / IPLD documentation.

cases.description: CIDv1 of the DAG-CBOR encoding of an empty map. Well-known IPLD reference vector.

verification.tool: pure-Python length-first DAG-CBOR encoder + sha2-256 + CIDv1 + base32-lower, implemented from the IPLD spec; cross-checked against the published js-multiformats dag-cbor reference value.

cases.description: CIDv1 of the DAG-CBOR encoding of {"hello":"world"}. Canonical IPLD reference vector.

verification.tool: pure-Python length-first DAG-CBOR encoder + sha2-256 + CIDv1 + base32-lower; cross-checked against the published js-multiformats dag-cbor reference value for {hello:world}.

cases.description: CIDv1 of a small descriptor-shaped map mixing string and integer values. Keys exercise length-first ordering across differing key byte-lengths (method(6), dataSize(8), interface(9), dataFormat(10)).

verification.tool: pure-Python length-first DAG-CBOR encoder + sha2-256 + CIDv1 + base32-lower, implemented from the IPLD spec.

cases.description: ORDERING-DIVERGENT vector. Keys "z" (1 byte) and "aa" (2 bytes) sort DIFFERENTLY under the two candidate orderings: length-first (canonical DAG-CBOR) emits "z" before "aa"; plain bytewise/lexicographic emits "aa" before "z". The expected literal below is the length-first canonical CID. A passing assertion proves the impl emits canonical length-first ordering, not lexicographic.

verification.tool: pure-Python length-first DAG-CBOR encoder + sha2-256 + CIDv1 + base32-lower, implemented from the IPLD spec / RFC 8949 §4.2.1.

cases.description: ORDERING-DIVERGENT vector, 3 keys. "b" (1 byte), "z" (1 byte) and "aa" (2 bytes). Length-first canonical order: b, z, aa. Lexicographic order would be: aa, b, z. The expected literal is the length-first canonical CID.

verification.tool: pure-Python length-first DAG-CBOR encoder + sha2-256 + CIDv1 + base32-lower, implemented from the IPLD spec / RFC 8949 §4.2.1.
